column7: Typo – This is a Print Change in order to correct the citation given here, which is definitively from the Mahābhārata (specifically, Droṇaparvan 7.159.42).

Typo – This is a Print Change in order to correct the spelling! Monier-Williams correctly prints (kumudabandhuḥ). The Proof: The substantive (bandha) denotes "binding," whereas (bandhu) denotes "friend." The contextual synonyms provided in the entry—(nātha), (pati), (bāndhava), and (suhṛd)—all denote "lord" or "friend," proving (kumudabandhaḥ) is a definitive typographical error for the intended (kumudabandhuḥ).
